programmes.show_description: Arthur's new gambling venture involves Terry taking on the prestigious task of guarding three public telephone boxes. S4 E4: Sorry Pal, Wrong Number
Alf, Mrs Hollingbery and Arthur are preparing for their trip to Australia. S4 E4
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
Several months have passed - Amy is working, but David is still unemployed and lies to her that his doctor has told him he will have a nervous breakdown unless he takes a holiday. S3 E1: Winter Break
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Lily and Walter find out they're not legally married. Walter stays with Nellie and Eli while he decides whether to marry and he's soon led astray. S3 E3: Get Up Them Stairs
The workers at the pickle factory go on strike when told they cannot use the new executive washroom. S3 E4: The Power Behind The Throne

programmes.show_name: Journeys: Isle of Man Steam Rail
programmes.show_description: Opened in 1873, the Isle of Man Steam Railway is the longest narrow gauge steam line in Britain that uses its original locomotives and carriages. We journey from Douglas to Port Erin.

Robin cooks a pork meal for Jo and her date not realising that her dinner guest is Jewish. S1 E3: Some Enchanted Evening
With Jo gone for the weekend, Chrissy frets over being alone in the flat with Robin. S1 E4: And Then There Were Two
Rigsby decides to move on, give up on Miss Jones and file for divorce - he places an advert in the local newspaper, but his search for love doesn't run entirely smoothly. S4 E4: Pink Carnations
New resident Ambrose claims he has the power of hypnosis due to his gypsy blood - Rigsby is sceptical but soon wonders if Ambrose's abilities could be used in his favour. S4 E5: Under The Influence
Rigsby finally pops the question to Miss Jones with his decree absolute at long last in hand, but will she accept? S4 E6: Come On In The Water's Lovely
Mavis Moulterd has still got a passionate crush on Mr Humphries. Mrs Slocombe gets a letter from an old friend, and Captain Peacock finds a loaded gun in the writing room. S2 E1
The staff continue the tradition of hosting and taking part in a cricket match, but preparations don't go quite as smoothly as expected. S2 E2
Mrs Slocombe is stunned when her long-lost husband appears to have made enquiries about buying the hotel... especially since he's been missing since he went to a supermarket in 1942. S2 E3
Captain Peacock discovers a dead cat hidden behind one of the walls - after the staff get rid of it, they find out that it was put in the hotel to cancel out bad luck. S2 E4
It seems that Mr Rumbold's wife has left him - which has made him more pompous - when he gets a vote of no confidence, and the staff agree to play a darts match with the villagers. S2 E5
A group of businessmen due to stay at the manor are expecting some culture, Shakespeare and opera, but there seems to be only one answer with no money to spend on entertainment. S2 E6
Tracey gets a letter from her auntie Vera who is retiring as a Pearly Queen and wants her to take over, but Tracey faces competition from a jealous Sharon. S11 E4: The Girls With The Pearl Buttons
Dorien's book group is convening to discuss Crime and Punishment, which turns out to be rather applicable to the events that unfold in Tracey's household. S11 E5: Tracey's Choice
Tracey is out of prison, and Sharon gets a job as a school dinner supervisor and recruits Garth as her cook and Tracey as her washer-up. S11 E6: The Chief, The Cook, His Mum and Her Lodger
Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap '70s weekender, but there is no dressing up soe news that Tracey is about to receive. S11 E7: Without a Trace
Tracey and Sharon take a trip to a spa to cope with Tracey's shattering news and try to make plans for the future, and Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter. S11 E8: Spa Wars
When Darryl dies, Tracey has to decide what kind of a send-off to give him - Sharon wants it to be nice for the boys and Dorien has got the perfect outfit. S12 E1: Knocking on Heaven's Door
Tracey's house has been burgled during Darryl's funeral, and the family decide that it's up to them to track down their possessions. S12 E2: Too Much of Nothing
Sharon's disapproval of Tracey dating policeman Barry and apparently overlooking Garth's feelings for his dad leads to a major quarrel between the sisters. S12 E3: Tombstone Blues
When Tracey has enough of Dorien entertaining gentlemen callers and Sharon not bringing in a living wage, she throws them both out and they end up in Sharon's old bedsit. S12 E4: Going, Going, Gone

Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in - there's only one room available, though. S12 E5: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here With You
A surprise visitor in the form of attractive Emma turns up at Tracey's house - Tracey is welcoming, Dorien is shocked to the core, and Travis is over the moon. S12 E6: Abandoned Love
When Travis has serious school news that he's scared to break to his mum, Sharon decides she'll pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. S12 E7: Blonde on Blonde
Arthur is forced to broadcast his first-ever commercial on pirate radio when a rival car dealer tries to put him out of business with a nightmare test drive.
The chips are down for Monty after Joyce sacks him and he needs a job fast - meanwhile, Kenneth wakes up in the Blow and Go and finds himself inadvertently bricked in! S10 E8
When Liam befriends a World War II veteran to help him with his school project, he finds solace in the old man's company, but Libby spies on them and jumps to all the wrong conclusions. S7 E11

Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding, but George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom
Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms; he's happy Miss Jones has returned but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy
The chips are down for Monty after Joyce sacks him and he needs a job fast - meanwhile, Kenneth wakes up in the Blow and Go and finds himself inadvertently bricked in! S10 E8
When an unscrupulous builder wants a piece of land occupied by a friend of Arthur's, he hires some thugs to put the frighteners on and tries to blame travellers. S4 E5: The Car Lot Baggers
Alf and Mrs Hollingbery are off to Australia to meet her rich brother and to get married. Alf is not sure which he is more frightened of - flying or getting married. S4 E5
Chrissy and Norman have been dating for some weeks to the annoyance of Robin, who goes on a disastrous double date with Larry. S6 E6: Fire Down Below
David is caught out when Amy tells him that Tommy Wright is dead, so he and Linda adopt disguises to avoid each other's spouses as they plan to move to another hotel. S3 E2: Deja Vu
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Eli is planning a weekend away while Nellie wants to decorate the cellar, but an error means that Eli and Nellie are stuck together for the weekend. S3 E5: Getting To Know You
With growing competition, the factory faces closure, leaving Eli and Nellie penniless, starving, and with no staff. S3 E6: Two Pennies To Rub Together
A trip on the Keighley & Worth Valley Railway, a five-mile branch line railway set in the heart of West Yorkshire that runs heritage trains through stunning Bronte Country.

A gambler dies owing Arthur Â£2000, but Terry has other problems to contend with when an old flame turns up. With guest star Billy Connolly. S7 E3: Fatal Impression
A night out gossiping with friends lands Edward in big trouble which could see him called as a witness in court. S3 E10: What the Footman Saw
Shifty departs under a cloud of suspicion, but Aveline and Oswald finally have a reason to celebrate. S6 E7
The battle lines are drawn between the Steptoes as a local by-election looms, and Albert is chosen to take tea with the Prime Minster to Harold's displeasure. S6 E4: Tea For Two
Ethel has fallen in love with an American pilot called Joe, but Wilfred is not at all pleased. S2 E6: Love is a Very Splendid Thing
Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, but he has not seen her yet... S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night
Chrissy's new boyfriend wants to take her to Bournemouth for the weekend, but Robin discovers something about him that he thinks might make Chrissy want to reconsider. S2 E3: In Praise of Older Men
Phoebe is due to give birth, but her cantankerous midwife is a little the worse for wear having had one too many drinks. S4 E11: The Bells Are Ringing
Tom meets a rich lady who is looking for her father and agrees to help; meanwhile, Billy starts work as a doctor at a local surgery in Wellesley Street. S2 E6: God & Love & Wellesley Street
A gambler dies owing Arthur Â£2000, but Terry has other problems to contend with when an old flame turns up. With guest star Billy Connolly. S7 E3: Fatal Impression
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny is slightly apprehensive about her decision to set up home with Vince again. S1 E7: Moving In and Out Again
While the flatmates compete to get the flat to themselves to entertain friends, George is trying to find jobs to avoid Mildred. S5 E4: Love and Let Love
Sitcom starring Michael Crawford. Frank causes chaos when he visits Betty in hospital. S1 E5: The Hospital Visit
Tracey, Sharon and Dorien each get romantically involved with three very different men, and Travis has romantic troubles of his own. S10 E4: Hearts For Sale
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
The battle lines are drawn between the Steptoes as a local by-election looms, and Albert is chosen to take tea with the Prime Minster to Harold's displeasure. S6 E4: Tea For Two
Rigsby is hoping to take new tenant Brenda out for the evening, but he has a cashflow problem and a string of unwelcome visitors who start arriving at the house. S2 E6: Last of the Big Spenders
When faced with a fight, Terry has a golden rule: 'Do unto others as they would do unto you - but do it first'. S2E12: Conduct Unbecoming
Arthur is being targeted by violent criminal Charlie Knowles, who is doing time for armed robbery and wants vengeance because Arthur sold him the getaway car.
A strike at the airport puts the anniversary party in jeopardy, but every cloud has a silver lining, as Billy meets his hero Holly Johnson! Sam, meanwhile, admits her feelings for Joey. S10 E9
Ian's attempts to raise funds for the local boxing club lead him into a hornet's nest of unfinished business between the club's owner and Paddy. S7 E12
The morning after a party, Chrissy and Jo find Robin asleep in their bath and ask him to become their third roommate. S1 E1: Three's a Crowd
Rigsby attempts to have himself cast as male lead in a play written by effeminate lodger Hilary when he discovers that Ruth is playing the role of the love interest. S3 E2: Stage Struck
